sourceMovie.title: RED 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit RED 2 for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Dean Parisot keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: RED
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If RED 2 clicked for you, RED (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Dark Knight Rises
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If RED 2 clicked for you, The Dark Knight Rises (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after RED 2?
faq.answer: Try RED, The Dark Knight Rises, Spy—each shares DNA with RED 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is RED 2?
faq.answer: RED 2 (2013) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is RED 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "RED Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
